title: RISC-V Studio
description: A complete RV32IMAFC + Zicsr assembler, cycle-stepping emulator, and time-travel debugger in the browser — with the compressed (C) extension (hand-written c.* or one-click auto-compression) and a full THREE-RING PRIVILEGE ARCHITECTURE: machine, supervisor and user mode. NEW: a real hardware Sv32 MMU — a two-level page-table walker (4 KiB pages and 4 MiB megapages) driven by satp, with V/R/W/X/U/G/A/D permission bits enforced against privilege plus SUM/MXR, hardware A/D-bit updates, a TLB fenced by sfence.vma, genuine page-fault exceptions (cause 12/13/15), MPRV-redirected M-mode accesses, and trap DELEGATION (medeleg/mideleg) that vectors faults to S-mode (stvec/sepc/scause/stval, sret) instead of M-mode. The register inspector renders the live page-table walk for the pc. All built on the existing machine-mode trap & interrupt architecture (mtvec/mepc/mcause, mret, a CLINT timer), a from-scratch C compiler that lowers a real subset of C to RISC-V, IEEE-754 float, atomics, CSR counters, a memory-mapped framebuffer, syscall console, disassembler, shareable links, and a 64-check in-app verification suite — all strictly additive, so with paging off the machine is byte-for-byte the bare-metal RV32 it always was.
